summary: The auriculotemporal nerve supplies sensation primarily to the posterior and inferior parts of the temporomandibular joint capsule. This information directly addresses which nerve innervates this specific area clinically significant for understanding pain pathways related to jaw function. Agent1 reasoned based on consistent medical literature stating the role of the auriculotemporal nerve in providing sensory innervation to these areas of the TMJ capsule. Answer provided was Option A due to its alignment with multiple reliable references highlighting the same anatomical fact.
